summary: Mandibular hypoplasia does not align with established diagnostic criteria for Apert Syndrome due to its absence among characteristic facial abnormalities including prominent bicoronal synostosis; instead, it’s more indicative of conditions where mid-face retrusion might be a primary finding but isn't part of classic Apert presentation. Symmetry in hand/foot webbing remains another critical distinguishing factor supporting non-inclusion here too. Sentence count maintained at two while emphasizing core aspects pertinent directly addressing differential diagnostics between Apert vs similar disorders based upon provided options. Agent2 concludes after thorough analysis using retrieved medical references confirming consistency across multiple authoritative resources about defining characteristics versus those less commonly associated specifically with Apert condition itself.
